text:
Assistant: Genuinely a good instinct — and worth saying first: the code is already ~80% living in this world. Per your own nouns, a project *is* a top-level NIP-29 group and a channel is the same group with a parent set. "Project" today is just the name for "channel whose parent is empty." So the honest question isn't "can we collapse them" — they're already collapsed — it's **"is there anything `project` carries that doesn't reduce to tree position?"** [...] > **A project is a channel that owns a workspace.** "Project" stops being a noun and becomes a *role* (or an attribute) a channel can have — the presence of a workspace binding, not a position in the tree. [...] ## 1. One node type: `channel`. "Project" becomes an attribute, not a concept.

A channel optionally carries a **workspace binding** (`machine + path`, i.e. what today makes something a project). One optional field on the node — explicitly *not* a subtype, not a different table, not a different code path. The word "project" survives only in human-facing rendering: a workspace-bearing channel gets shown as a project root. Everything in the schema, resolver, and daemon speaks only `channel`. [...] ## 3. Workspace binding resolves by **nearest ancestor**.

An agent in `tenex-edge/reactivity/perf-hunt` asks "where's my repo?" → walk up to the nearest workspace-bearing ancestor. This one rule is what makes monorepo sub-projects free later (a child channel binding a subdirectory just shadows the root binding) — but you don't have to build multi-binding now. Ship with "root binds, descendants inherit," and the recursion is already correct. [...] - **No deep nesting encouragement.** The model supports arbitrary depth; the culture shouldn't. Two levels (root + task rooms) covers ~all real use, and your per-session-rooms-optional decision already points that way. Depth is a capability you get for free, not a feature to promote.
